title: Sivaraman's Conjecture
statement_text: Two cops can win on connected $P_5$-free graphs; more generally, the cop number of connected $P_t$-free graphs is at most $t-3$ for $t \geq 5$.
context_text: Joret et al. had previously shown that the cop number of connected $P_t$-free graphs is at most $t-2$ for $t \geq 3$. Sivaraman conjectured the improved bound of $t-3$ for $t \geq 5$, with the $P_5$-free case attracting the most attention; various partial results (for subclasses of $P_5$-free graphs, and for the weaker 2K2-free class) had been established before the present work.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full for $t = 5$: every connected $P_5$-free graph has cop number at most two.
